from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a politician into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple phrase generators for each predicate
    def phrase(subj: str, pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
        pred_map = {
            "battle": f"{subj} fought in the {obj}",
            "birthPlace": f"{subj} was born in {obj}",
            "deathPlace": f"{subj} died in {obj}",
            "almaMater": f"{subj} studied at {obj}",
            "birthDate": f"{subj} was born on {obj}",
            "nationality": f"{subj} is a {obj} national",
            "office": f"{subj} served as {obj}",
            "party": f"{subj} was a member of the {obj} party",
            "hasDeputy": f"{subj} had {obj} as deputy",
            "region": f"{subj} represented the region of {obj}",
            "residence": f"{subj} lived at {obj}",
            "successor": f"{subj} was succeeded by {obj}",
            "country": f"{subj} is from {obj}",
            "inOfficeWhileVicePresident": f"{subj} served while {obj} was vice‑president",
            "militaryBranch": f"{subj} served in the {obj}",
            "inOfficeWhilePrimeMinister": f"{subj} served while {obj} was prime minister",
            "inOfficeWhilePresident": f"{subj} served while {obj} was president",
            "spouse": f"{subj} was married to {obj}",
            "activeYearsStartDate": f"{subj} started active years on {obj}",
            "numberOfVotesAttained": f"{subj} attained {obj} votes",
            "award": f"{subj} received the {obj}",
            "activeYearsEndDate": f"{subj} ended active years on {obj}",
            "unit": f"{subj} belonged to the unit {obj}",
            "birthYear": f"{subj} was born in {obj}",
            "deathDate": f"{subj} died on {obj}",
            "deathYear": f"{subj} died in {obj}",
            "profession": f"{subj} was a {obj}",
            "religion": f"{subj} practiced {obj}",
            "commander": f"{subj} was commanded by {obj}",
            "language": f"The language of {subj} is {obj}",
            "officialLanguage": f"The official language of {subj} is {obj}",
            "leader": f"The leader of {subj} is {obj}",
            "isPartOfMilitaryConflict": f"The {subj} was part of the {obj}",
            "governingBody": f"The governing body of {subj} is {obj}",
            "affiliation": f"{obj} is affiliated with {subj}",
            "predecessor": f"{subj} succeeded {obj}",
            "inOfficeWhileGovernor": f"{subj} served while {obj} was governor",
            "currency": f"The currency of {subj} is {obj}",
            "colour": f"The colour of {subj} is {obj}",
            "trainerAircraft": f"{subj} used the trainer aircraft {obj}",
            "ethnicGroup": f"The ethnic group of {subj} is {obj}",
            "garrison": f"The garrison of {subj} is {obj}",
            "capital": f"The capital of {subj} is {obj}",
            "largestCity": f"The largest city of {subj} is {obj}",
            "place": f"The {subj} took place in {obj}",
            "inOfficeWhileMonarch": f"{subj} served while {obj} was monarch",
            "militaryRank": f"{subj} held the rank of {obj}",
            "serviceStartYear": f"{subj} started service in {obj}",
            "state": f"{subj} is a state of {obj}",
        }
        return pred_map.get(pred, f"{subj} {pred} {obj}")

    # Build clause list
    clauses = [phrase(t.subject, t.predicate, t.object) for t in triples]

    # Join clauses into a single sentence with better readability
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    elif len(clauses) == 2:
        sentence = " and ".join(clauses) + "."
    else:
        # Use semicolons to separate intermediate clauses for clarity
        sentence = "; ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
